calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($195,232 USD / 19,553 total beneficiaries) = $9.98 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 100 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: ["Quote: The Blue Cross of Hyderabad systematically implements the Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV) programs across multiple municipalities.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Policy And Legal Advocacy'", "EA Rationale: Policy and legal advocacy can create systemic, legally-binding protections for animals, representing a highly tractable path to impact."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'My gratitude for the timely care provided to 19553 animals, through life saving vaccinations, Veterinary interventions and critical care.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 0%, Wild Animals: 0%, Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

significant_events.source_quote: The Blue Cross of Hyderabad systematically implements the Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination (ARV) programs across multiple municipalities.
significant_events.summary: Systematic implementation of Animal Birth Control and Anti-Rabies Vaccination programs across multiple municipalities in Hyderabad and Secunderabad Districts, Medchal-Malkajgiri District, Ranga Reddy District, Sangareddy District, and Medak District.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad launched a new Animal Birth Control (ABC) project at the Cantonment area on December 1st, 2024.
significant_events.summary: Launched a new Animal Birth Control project in the Cantonment area to manage street dog population through sterilization and vaccination.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad begins operations under the Toopran Project initiative. Marking the launch with the signing of the MOU, dogs were caught for ABC/ ARV.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of Hyderabad began operations under the Toopran Project initiative, signing an MOU to address street animal population and welfare.

significant_events.source_quote: Launching the second project in Shankarpally municipality, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work, broadening our footprint in suburban areas that need attention.
significant_events.summary: Launched the second project in Shankarpally municipality, conducting sterilization, vaccination, and outreach work, broadening footprint in suburban areas.

significant_events.event_name: ABC Veterinary Training for Government Vets
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad and Worldwide Veterinary Service conducted specialized ABC surgery training for two batches of Government Vets, training 16 veterinarians in modern welfare practices for street dogs.
significant_events.summary: Conducted specialized ABC surgery training for 16 Government Vets in modern welfare practices for street dogs, strengthening grassroots veterinary care.

significant_events.source_quote: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, was led by CSR team- Vamshi Muthyapu & Padmaja in Pocharam region. 500 dogs were covered in areas of Korremulla and Chowdariguda.
significant_events.summary: A 5-month ABC & Vaccination project sponsored by Infosys, covering 500 dogs in Pocharam region, demonstrating corporate partnership in animal welfare.

significant_events.event_name: Secunderabad Awareness Drive & Water Bowl Distribution
significant_events.source_quote: As part of our community outreach initiative, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a series of awareness programs across the Secunderabad region... distributed water bowls to households, shopkeepers, and animal feeders...
significant_events.summary: Community outreach initiative in Secunderabad to sensitize the public on animal welfare issues and distribute water bowls for strays.

significant_events.event_name: School Awareness Programs on Animal Welfare
significant_events.source_quote: We conducted awareness sessions in schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and the importance of animal birth control.
significant_events.summary: Conducted awareness sessions in Narsingi Government schools to educate students on responsible pet care, rabies prevention, and animal birth control.

significant_events.event_name: Awareness Camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College
significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted an awareness camp at Gokaraju Rangaraju College, focusing on key issues such as animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and the ABC/ARV programs.
significant_events.summary: Conducted an awareness camp focusing on animal welfare, zoonotic diseases, vaccinations, and ABC/ARV programs for students.

significant_events.source_quote: Adoptions are organized on a dedicated Adoption Day held on the last Sunday of every month, helping to find loving homes for these young animals and reducing the number of strays on streets.
significant_events.summary: Organized monthly adoption days for puppies brought in with whelping mother dogs for ABC surgery, ensuring they find loving homes.

significant_events.event_name: World Rabies Prevention Day Vaccination Camp
significant_events.source_quote: On the occasion of World Rabies Prevention Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad conducted a successful vaccination camp, where an impressive 4,415 dogs were vaccinated with the support of WVS and Mission Rabies over a fortnight leading to the event.
significant_events.summary: Conducted a successful vaccination camp, vaccinating 4,415 dogs, to raise awareness about rabies prevention and protect animals.

significant_events.source_quote: A large-scale community geo-tagging, for the first time in Hyderabad and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive was conducted in Manikonda, covering over 812 street dogs.
significant_events.summary: Large-scale community geo-tagging and Anti-Rabies Vaccination drive covering over 812 street dogs in Manikonda.

significant_events.source_quote: On World Zoonosis Day, Blue Cross of Hyderabad organized a vaccination camp, successfully vaccinating 355 dogs.
significant_events.summary: Organized a vaccination camp, vaccinating 355 dogs, to raise awareness about zoonotic diseases and ensure animal/community health.

significant_events.source_quote: Radio Mirchi teamed up with Blue Cross of Hyderabad for a large-scale adoption drive drawing attention to shelter animals who need homes.
significant_events.summary: Teamed up with Radio Mirchi for a large-scale adoption drive, connecting adopters with well-cared-for vaccinated pets.

significant_events.source_quote: The ABC team of Blue cross of Hyderabad was selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai.The program focused on vaccination and field skills enhancement with geo tagging.
significant_events.summary: ABC team selected for WVS advanced training in Mumbai, focusing on vaccination and field skills enhancement with geo tagging.

significant_events.source_quote: The Blue Cross team travelled to Goa for further training under WVS. They gained hands-on experience in welfare interventions.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross team travelled to Goa for further training under WVS, gaining hands-on experience in welfare interventions and sharing field experiences.

significant_events.source_quote: A public rally was conducted from Blue Cross of Hyderabad to KBR Park with the goal to spread awareness on animal welfare and health.
significant_events.summary: A public rally conducted from Blue Cross of Hyderabad to KBR Park to spread awareness on animal welfare and health.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad proudly organized the "Love My Indie Dog" Show, a heartwarming event dedicated to celebrating the charm and resilience of Indian street dogs.
significant_events.summary: Event celebrating Indian street dogs, promoting adoption, responsible pet ownership, and the bond between humans and companions.

significant_events.source_quote: Blue Cross of Hyderabad received the award for service in animal welfare.
significant_events.summary: Blue Cross of Hyderabad received an award for service in animal welfare, recognizing their impactful community work.

significant_events.source_quote: The Annual Animal Welfare Board of India Inspection was conducted by JD - Dr. Venkateshwarlu, Animal Husbandry Dept., Sitarambagh
significant_events.summary: The Annual Animal Welfare Board of India Inspection was conducted by JD - Dr. Venkateshwarlu, Animal Husbandry Dept., Sitarambagh.

significant_events.source_quote: An inspection was conducted to assess the Infosys project. The findings validated the program's success and outreach, reinforcing the model for future collaborations.
significant_events.summary: An inspection was conducted to assess the Infosys project, validating its success and outreach, reinforcing the model for future collaborations.

data.original_text: Blue Cross of Hyderabad is a non-profit organization dedicated to street animal welfare.
Their mission is to focus on animal birth control, vaccinations, and animal welfare training.
They work in alliance with the GHMC to implement the Animal Birth Control Anti-Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in specific areas of the city.
The organization also provides basic checkups, vaccinations, and emergency care for sick and injured animals.
They offer training for volunteers, veterinarians, pet owners, and animal welfare groups.
Adoption events are held monthly to facilitate the adoption of healthy puppies and kittens.
The organization does not accept abandoned animals or house pets for boarding and encourages the use of government veterinary hospitals and GHMC animal shelters.
To support their cause, visitors can donate, sponsor, volunteer, or become a member through their official website.
Located in Hyderabad, Telangana, the Blue Cross of Hyderabad operates from a two-acre animal shelter in Jubilee Hills.
The shelter is surrounded by hills and vast open spaces, housing over 650 animals of different species.

The organisation prevents cruelty to animals, rescues the sick and the injured, shelters them, provides mobile clinics, educates people on animal welfare and carries out many more animal welfare activities that upholds the existence of these simple souls.
To reduce the number of homeless stray animals in the streets, Blue Cross vaccinates over 15,000 stray dogs, against rabies, each year.
The organisation sterilises around 600 street dogs and cats every month and vaccinates them against rabies.
It also conducts extensive adoption drives to find homes for stray puppies and kittens to reduce the number of homeless strays in the streets.
Many who semi own dogs but cannot afford Veterinary treatment, become allies in the dog management of the area.

Blue Cross aims to reach a large target audience within the community so they support the Animal Birth Control/Anti-Rabies Vaccination efforts, how to behave around animals and thereby create safe communities.
Our Animal Welfare Training Centre facilitates training for Veterinarians, pet owners, animal welfare groups and students.
We conduct an effective Animal Birth Control/ Anti Rabies Vaccination program for stray dogs in West Zone, Jubilee and Banjara Hills, 15 kms around our location.
In 2016, BCH trained 114 government vets from Andhra Pradesh, organising a three-week training program-me that was conducted in six batches.

The annual report summarizes the activities of Blue Cross of Hyderabad for 2019-2020. It highlights that 11,409 animals were helped over the year, including 9,708 that underwent animal birth control operations.

During the financial year of 2018-19, Blue Cross of Hyderabad was able to provide help to 10,844 animals; of which 8198 were through the Animal Birth Control /Anti Rabies Vaccination programme, and the rest were treated in emergency care, at our clinic and through our adoption drives.
Our effort proved to be extremely successful to reduce stray dog population on the street and thereby reduce animal suffering exponentially.

We proved this by actually seeing a reduction in animals in accidents, less puppies dying of parvo and distemper, and more people helping animals; while ABC operated dogs are being accepted by their communities in the areas we work.
This mature and responsible attitude has boosted the number of people and groups helping animals to a new record.
Since inception through services and efforts, Blue Cross of Hyderabad has helped over 5,35,790 animals.

The pandemic year was completely handled by Mrs.kumari , our CFO and Our Veterinarians Dr.Shubam, Dr.Haritha, Dr.Irshad, supported by their teams of dedicated staff. We stayed focused on Animal Birth Control and Rabies control efforts.
The impressive numbers of animals helped, 38,640 to be precise, are due to a well coordinated team effort established over the years.
To highlight the work of our ambulance and rescue service, 8500 animals were rescued, sick, injured and a bused from the streets.
